lede: A look at May 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Franco Colapinto and Irina Davydova, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Kerala Blasters FC
events.desc: The football club Kerala Blasters FC and its first supporters' group Manjappada are formed.

events.title: 2006 Yogyakarta earthquake
events.desc: The 6.4 Mw Yogyakarta earthquake shakes central Java with an MSK intensity of VIII (Damaging), leaving more than 5,700 dead and 37,000 injured.

events.title: 1967 Australian referendum (Aboriginals)
events.desc: Australians vote in favor of a constitutional referendum granting the Australian government the power to make laws to benefit Indigenous Australians and to count them in the national census.

events.title: McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II
events.desc: First flight of the McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In the Le Paradis massacre, 99 soldiers from a Royal Norfolk Regiment unit are shot after surrendering to German troops; two survive.

events.title: Supreme Court of the United States
events.desc: New Deal: The Supreme Court of the United States declares the National Industrial Recovery Act to be unconstitutional in A.L.A. Schechter Poultry Corp. v. United States, (295 U.S. 495).

events.title: Chrysler Building
events.desc: The 1,046 feet (319 m) Chrysler Building in New York City, the tallest man-made structure at the time, opens to the public.

events.title: HMS Princess Irene
events.desc: HMS Princess Irene explodes and sinks off Sheerness, Kent, with the loss of 352 lives.

events.title: War of the Second Coalition
events.desc: War of the Second Coalition: Austrian forces defeat the French at Winterthur, Switzerland.

events.title: Manchu people
events.desc: Manchu regent Dorgon defeats rebel leader Li Zicheng of the Shun dynasty at the Battle of Shanhai Pass, allowing the Manchus to enter and conquer the capital city of Beijing.

births.name: Denise van Outen
births.line: English actress, singer, and television host

births.name: Mal Evans
births.line: British road manager of The Beatles (died 1976)

births.name: Terry Moore (baseball)
births.line: American baseball player, coach, and manager (died 1995)

deaths.text: Simeon Daniel, Nevisian educator and politician, 1st Premier of Nevis
Lois Lamya al-Faruqi, Islamic scholar of ethnomusicology
Gordon Coates, New Zealand soldier and politician, 21st Prime Minister of New Zealand
